001.title: Born 1981. That's the claim.
001.desc: Day 001 — origin, place, the Suwan Panit shop registration (พ.ศ. ๒๕๑๒ / 1969 CE), the 發祥 Chinese mercantile hao, and the Pantip-Plaza-as-Doric-columns childhood. Twenty-seven claims; one contradiction (Siam Paragon, 1981).
001.telemetry_note: 999 words across 2 messages · 2026-05-25 Asia/Bangkok · 27 claims extracted

artifact_captions.caption: Thai Commercial Registration Certificate · ร้านสุวรรณพานิช · Sikhio, Nakhon Ratchasima · พ.ศ. ๒๕๑๒ / 1969 CE
Suwan Panit shop frontage today · concrete post-fire rebuild · house no. 186 · the Chinese hao 發祥 visible on the signboard

002.title: I made it.
002.desc: Day 002 — the mother, the father's death sequence, the Jamsuan maternal name encoded in the C initial, the 3-million baht university-ties payoff, Paolo the pug named after Paulo Mendes da Rocha, and the closing reflection: I made it.

death_ledger.who: Maternal grandfather (Agong) — Non receives the news at the Stanford Bookstore, waiting for Laura.
Maternal grandmother (Ama) — passes not long after Agong.
Father — Kongkiet Arkaraprasertkul. First day of Non's class at Harvard.
Paolo the pug — five years old. Non was with Riley in New York. Paolo did not know Dad had passed; he was still waiting.
Paternal grandfather — funeral hall, ~7 mourners. "The most disliked person in the family."

artifact_captions.caption: Paternal grandfather's funeral · ~2020 · Thai temple funeral hall · the most disliked person in the family · all the cousins were there
Father's ashes site · the hill that looks like the Windows XP screensaver · he was attached to that version of Windows
Paolo the pug · 2008–2013 · gift from a Silpakorn-University ex-girlfriend on Non's MIT graduation · named after Paulo Mendes da Rocha · Pritzker 2006

003.title: People were wrong.
003.desc: Day 003 — the father, deep arc. 10 messages, 16 artifacts. The canonical civil-servant portrait. The MIT final visit on a wheelchair. The Statue of Liberty father-son photo. Japan 2006 with Kengo Kuma. The 3-million-baht grant trap and the father paying it off to set Non free. The lifelong devotion of routing every salary to mother as repayment. And the anaphoric climax: PEOPLE WERE WRONG.
003.telemetry_note: 10 messages · 16 artifacts · 2026-05-26 → 27 Asia/Bangkok · the longest day filed yet · fact-check pending

artifact_captions.caption: Kongkiet Arkaraprasertkul · full Thai high-ranking civil-servant white ceremonial dress · shoulder boards · full chest of decorations · canonical reference image for the father across the project

Father and mother at MIT · Killian Court · the Great Dome · the trip Non describes: "he told the doctor he just wanted to come to visit me one more time, even on a wheelchair." Father standing here — the determination to make the trip is what the photo carries.

anaphora.end: I WASN'T SMART ENOUGH FOR HARVARD.
DR. NON ENDS UP A FOOTNOTE.

003.coda: I have a good health. I don't have a healthy problem at all. I'm happy with my, with my health. I don't have a problem with money at all. I don't have problem with anything at all. I'm happy with lots of things, right? I just got home, so we'll continue this later.
